title: Living in Boulder vlog 8 - 29 - 18: History Books

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a video log update from August 29, 2018, covering his ongoing project to organize 264 history and philosophy books on his website with chapter-level page and time metadata. He also discusses receiving a treadmill, his plans to start streaming his daily life as a triathlete and content creator, and the technical challenges of recording this video (his third attempt due to messy background and bad audio).
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: streaming is the most revolutionary technology for humanity, comparable to Columbus connecting the hemispheres, and almost no one is using it correctly
people waste streaming potential on video games instead of documenting healthy, athletic, intellectual lives
prefers computer-read audiobooks for speed but acknowledges human narration adds warmth and helps retention
deeply committed to tagging every chapter with page count and timestamp across 264 books so he can relocate anything he hears
multitasking is essential and industrializing the mind; filming from bed while resting is the only practical way to do video when training full time

entities: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked all night long and recorded a video at 7 in the morning before going to sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I ordered a treadmill since the last time I did a video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was waiting for my Elgato stream deck to arrive in the mail, but it didn't come on time because Amazon denied me due to unpaid bills.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to pay double the amount to Amazon to reactivate my account.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I ended up ordering the Elgato from Best Buy and had to wait an extra week for it to arrive.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I focused on my history books because I want to know exactly where I am in the book while listening.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to number all the chapters and attach pages and time to them for 121,000 pages over 264 books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I found the Quran online last month after it wasn't available before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on Platner and Ashby, but it's extremely slow, taking hours to process.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm also working on St. Augustine's 'City of God,' which has 699 chapters, and I'm labeling each chapter with its pages and time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I set my reading speed to 50 seconds per page on Natural Reader, which is the fastest setting on Mac.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I prefer listening to computer-read books because it's faster, but I also enjoy human-read books for their inflections.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a treadmill for $275 that goes up to 10 miles per hour.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I also bought a foot sensor to track my running speed for Zwift.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I spent $150 on a Garmin light that came with a stand and mount.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to start running on Zwift once I receive the foot sensor.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I messed up the numbering in some of my books and need to fix it, which will take a few days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is the third time I've recorded this video because the first two had issues with messiness and sound quality.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I now have good sound quality for my videos after fixing my mic setup.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to start streaming my daily life, including sleeping, waking up, and recapping my dreams.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to stream my morning routine, including prayers to Mecca since I identify as Muslim.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to stream my breakfast while watching and discussing the news.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to stream my workouts, including swimming and biking, with live stats like speed and heart rate.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to configure my stream so that Instagram posts or tweets automatically appear in my YouTube videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to stream myself stretching while listening to something, sharing what I'm listening to in real time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I'm still smoking weed, I might stream myself dancing and singing while high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to monetize my streaming by showing how much money I make daily from my website and YouTube.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't received a check from Google in over a year because I got blackballed from YouTube.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I may have been blackballed from my website because I used too many other people's photos without paying.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need $200 a month for a Dreamstime subscription to legally use photos on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm $16,000 in debt and considering starting a Patreon to help cover costs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My ultimate goal is to become a triathlete and compete in races.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't uploaded a video in 18 days because I've been focused on organizing my history books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to check back in a week to update on my progress with the books.
